entry.title: Micro.blog - First Impressions
entry.content:
Now that I have a blog with an RSS feed, I want to check out Micro.blog. It’s yet-another-Twitter-replacement, but unlike others I’ve tried, this one isn’t based on new protocols or technology, instead it relies on a rather old one - RSS.

All I had to do to get started was register at Micro.blog and plug in my RSS feed’s URL. This allows me to follow people on the network and see their posts and replies in a timeline.

My first problem was finding friends. I didn’t see an easy way to search for my Twitter friends on micro.blog. In fact, I couldn’t search for my friends at all. If you know someone’s username you can type it in and follow them. Otherwise the recommended way to find people is by looking at the Discover timeline, which contains posts from a “random selection of featured users”.

My second problem was finding out that my replies on micro.blog will stay on micro.blog. This makes perfect sense given that it is really just syndicating RSS feeds, but imo it really falls short of the dream. For a service that “encourages publishing at your own domain name, where you can control your own content” it seems weird that a large chunk of the content is hosted and controlled by yet another centralized service.

So my first impression is that this is not the service that will save the web or be the social network replacement I am looking for, but it may be a nice water cooler to hang out at in the meantime.
entry.summary: Now that I have a blog with an RSS feed, I want to check out Micro.blog. It’s yet-another-Twitter-replacement, but unlike others I’ve tried, this one isn’t based on new protocols or technology, instead it relies on a rather old one - RSS.

entry.title: This Is The Beginning Of The Blog

link.title: This Is The Beginning Of The Blog

entry.content:
This is the beginning of my blog. It is made with Jekyll. It is not federated or self-hosted, but I would like it to be. It might be more of a traditional blog, or more of the micro type. I’m not sure yet.

I want to use it as a journal of personal projects and a place to remember things that I think are cool. I would like to interact with my friends and family through it, but I’m not sure how I want that to happen just yet. Instead of waiting until I’ve figured out exactly what I will do with my blog, I shipped it. It will never be perfect, and if I wait until I know it will be it will probably never get made.
entry.summary: This is the beginning of my blog. It is made with Jekyll. It is not federated or self-hosted, but I would like it to be. It might be more of a traditional blog, or more of the micro type. I’m not sure yet.
